name: Onion-Parmesan Bread Sticks
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the water and onions in a quart saucepan. Let stand for 5 minutes to soften the onions. Heat mixture over medium heat until hot. Remove from heat.
2. Combine the hot roll mix and oil in a large mixing bowl. Stir in the onion mixture until combined. Knead dough on lightly floured surface for 5 minutes, or until smooth (add flour as necessary to reduce stickiness). Cover dough with bowl. Let rest for 5 minutes.
3. Lightly spray two or three baking sheets with nonstick vegetable cooking spray. Set aside.
4. Preheat oven to 375°F. Roll dough into a 16x14 inch rectangle. Cut half crosswise into 1-inch strips. Twist each strip 3-4 times.
5. Arrange strips on prepared baking sheets. Cover with lightly greased plastic wrap. Let rise in warm place until doubled in size (about 20 minutes). Remove plastic wrap. Brush bread sticks with egg. Sprinkle evenly with Parmesan cheese. Bake, one sheet at a time, for 12-15 minutes, or until bread sticks are golden brown.
